title: [Feature]: Add subject-aware observability policy model
problem: Operators may need subject-family visibility, but the current observability policy intentionally does not export subjects. Adding subject labels without a dedicated policy model would risk sensitive metadata disclosure and unbounded metric cardinality.
proposal: Add a disabled-by-default subject-aware observability policy block that supports default-deny behavior, explicit subject-family allow rules, stable operator-chosen labels, bounded maximum subject families, deterministic overflow behavior, and optional hash/redaction display modes. The policy should reject raw subject sharing unless an explicit reviewed mode allows it.
users: Operators who need controlled subject-family observability, security reviewers, and maintainers extending the observability connector model.
delivery_semantics: Subject-aware policy validation must not affect message delivery. Invalid or disabled observability policy must fail closed for export while leaving ACK behavior, sink writes, retries, and DLQ handling unchanged.
security: Subjects are controlled metadata. The policy must be disabled by default, reject high-cardinality or secret-looking labels, validate NATS subject patterns with the shared matcher, avoid payload and credential values, and document that hashing is not a confidentiality boundary.
acceptance: Subject-aware export remains disabled by default.
Operator-chosen labels are validated and bounded.
Cardinality caps and overflow actions are explicit.
Invalid subject-aware policy fails closed without changing delivery behavior.
tests: Add unit tests for disabled default, allow rules, deny rules, invalid subject patterns, invalid labels, cardinality cap validation, overflow action validation, hash/redaction mode validation, and unchanged aggregate policy behavior. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update subject-aware observability evaluation, observability, metrics, Prometheus, security, roadmap, and changelog documentation.
closeout: Close after the release containing the subject-aware observability policy model is published. Include release tag, tests, documentation, and limitations.
